column1: ## All values at 298.15 K unless otherwise noted
## Phenolic OH...O hydrogen bond to β-CD peripheral hydroxyls
## HEAT CAPACITY DATA (Table 3 and text p1910)
## 1. CH2 inclusion is ENTHALPY-DRIVEN: avg d∆G°/dNC ≈ d∆H°/dNC for both α and β
## 2. Entropy term CONTROLS ultimate stability (can cancel or enhance enthalpic gain)
## 3. d∆G°/dNC ≈ -3 kJ/mol matches water→hexane transfer (hydrophobic effect)
## 4. d∆H°/dNC ≈ -3.3 kJ/mol is MORE negative than water→nonpolar transfer (≥-1.5)
##    → van der Waals contacts in cavity contribute beyond classical hydrophobic effect
## 5. Slope remains constant up to NC=10 despite cavity depth only 7.9 Å
## 6. Only the part of guest INSIDE cavity contributes to thermodynamics
##    → hydrophilic groups outside cavity do NOT affect ΔG°
